prompt:
The web-prod deploy fails because the Fly app gunk-web-prod doesn't exist yet. The registry rejects pushes with 'app repository not found'.

## Fix

Add an 'Ensure app exists' step BEFORE the 'Load and push OCI image' step in .github/workflows/web-prod.yml:

```yaml
      - name: Ensure app exists
        env:
          FLY_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.FLY_API_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          if ! nix develop -c fly apps list --org gunk-dev | grep -q gunk-web-prod; then
            nix develop -c fly apps create gunk-web-prod --org gunk-dev
          fi
```

This matches the pattern in scripts/deploy.sh (lines 56-58).

Also add the same pattern to prod.yml (flux) and staging.yml if they don't already have it — any workflow that pushes to the Fly registry needs the app to exist first.

## File
- .github/workflows/web-prod.yml — add ensure app step before image push

## Acceptance criteria
- App creation step runs before registry push
- Idempotent (doesn't fail if app already exists)
- CI passes
